core.label: Common Lisp Fundamentals
core.description: S-expressions, functions, macros, and the REPL.

topics.label: Installation (SBCL)
topics.description: Install SBCL and Quicklisp. Common Lisp is the most feature-rich Lisp dialect.

topics.label: S-expressions & Evaluation
topics.description: Everything is (function arg1 arg2). Prefix notation and code-as-data (homoiconicity).

topics.label: Variables & Types
topics.description: defvar, defparameter, let, let*. Dynamic and lexical scoping. Dynamic typing.

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: defun, lambda, funcall, apply. Functions are first-class values.

topics.label: Lists & Cons Cells
topics.description: cons, car, cdr, list. Lists are built from cons cells — the fundamental data structure.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if, cond, when, unless, case, loop. The LOOP macro is powerful and complex.

topics.label: Macros
topics.description: defmacro for code generation. Lisp macros transform code at compile time. Lisp's defining feature.

topics.label: CLOS (Object System)
topics.description: defclass, defmethod, defgeneric. CLOS is the most flexible OOP system in any language.

topics.label: Conditions & Restarts
topics.description: condition system with handler-case, handler-bind, restart-case. More powerful than try/catch.

topics.label: Packages & Systems
topics.description: defpackage for namespaces, ASDF for system definitions. Organize large projects.
